title: carex vulgaris subsp. elytroides
intro: Carex vulgaris subsp. elytroides, also known as the eastern yellow-eyed grass, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the Carex genus. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Carex vulgaris subsp. elytroides include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and support digestive health. Carex vulgaris subsp. elytroides can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also sometimes used in traditional folk remedies, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
